case_title: KEVIN STRAIN VS. SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT STABIZATION AND et al
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: REPLY BRIEF IN SUPPORT OF WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDMUS FILED BY PETITIONER STRAIN, KEVIN

PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION FILED BY PETITIONER STRAIN, KEVIN AS TO RESPONDENT SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT STABIZATION AND ARBITRATION BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DOES 1 THROUGH 25 JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED
MINI-MINUTES FOR DEC-11-2008 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER KEVIN STRAIN'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS IS DENIED. SECTION 1.21 OF THE RENT ORDINANCE REQUIRES A REVIEW OF SEVERAL FACTORS TO DETERMINE A TENANTS PRINCIPAL RESIDENCE. THE PARTIES INTRODUCED EVIDENCE SUPPORTING THEIR RESPECTIVE POSITIONS AND THE RECORD IS MIXED WHEN THE ENUMERATED CRITERIA ARE CONSIDERED. PETITIONER HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THE ADMINSTRATIVE LAW JUDGE'S DECISION IS AN ABUSE OF DISCRETION UNDER THE SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE STANDARD. ALTHOUGH THE TENANT'S TAX RETURNS AND GRANT DEED ARE PROBATIVE, THE ADMINSTRATIVE LAW JUDGE PROPERLY GAVE WEIGHT TO CHILD SUPPORT DOCUMENTS, AND BANK DOCUMENTS AS WELL AS OTHER FACTORS CITED. ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD AND TRANSCRIPT RETURNED ON THE RECORD TO PETITIONER. RESPONDENT TO PREPARE PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Notice Of Motion For Writ Of Administrtive Mandamus
Off Calendar Dec-11-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER KEVIN STRAIN'S Motion For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus.
APPEARANCES: John P. Zanghi, Esq. of Zanghi Torres Arshawsky LLP 977-0444 for petitioner & moving party; Wayne K. Snodgrass, Deputy City Atty 554-4675 for respondent & opposing party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENIED. SECTION 1.21 OF THE RENT ORDINANCE REQUIRES A REVIEW OF SEVERAL FACTORS TO DETERMINE A TENANTS PRINCIPAL RESIDENCE. THE PARTIES INTRODUCED EVIDENCE SUPPORTING THEIR RESPECTIVE POSITIONS AND THE RECORD IS MIXED WHEN THE ENUMERATED CRITERIA ARE CONSIDERED. PETITIONER HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THE ADMINSTRATIVE LAW JUDGE'S DECISION IS AN ABUSE OF DISCRETION UNDER THE SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE STANDARD. ALTHOUGH THE TENANT'S TAX RETURNS AND GRANT DEED ARE PROBATIVE, THE ADMINSTRATIVE LAW JUDGE PROPERLY GAVE WEIGHT TO CHILD SUPPORT DOCUMENTS, AND BANK DOCUMENTS AS WELL AS OTHER FACTORS CITED. Administrative record and transcript returned on the record to petitioner. Respondent to prepare proposed form of order. (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
